return connection\n\n    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n            del self.connections[connection_id]\n\n    def heartbeat(self, connection_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Update heartbeat for a connection. Returns True if connection exists.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            self.connections[connection_id].last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    async def broadcast(self, event: SSEEvent) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast an event to relevant connections.\n\n        Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n        \"\"\"\n        sent_count = 0\n\n        for conn in list(self.connections.values()):\n            # Filter by target\n            if event.target_session_id and conn.session_id != event.target_session_id:\n                continue\n            if event.target_surface_id and conn.surface_id != event.target_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if event.exclude_surface_id and conn.surface_id == event.exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n\n            # Queue the event\n            try:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(event)\n                sent_count += 1\n            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                logger.warning(f\"Queue full for connection {conn.connection_id}, dropping event\")\n\n        return sent_count\n\n    async def event_generator(self, connection: SSEConnection):\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate SSE events for a connection.\n\n        Yields formatted SSE messages.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield self._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"connection_id\": connection.connection_id,\n                \"surface_id\": connection.surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": connection.session_id,\n            })\n\n            while True:\n                event = await connection.queue.get()\n                connection.last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n\n                # Format and yield the event\n                yield self._format_sse(event.event_type, event.data)\n\n                # Special case: disconnect event ends the stream\n                if event.event_type == \"disconnect\":\n                    logger.info(f\"Disconnect event for connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    break\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"Connection {connection.connection_id} cancelled\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.unregister(connection.connection_id)\n\n    def _format_sse(self, event_type: str, data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format an event as SSE.\"\"\"\n        return f\"event: {event_type}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n\n    async def _cleanup_loop(self):\n        \"\"\"Periodically clean up dead connections.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await asyncio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds\n\n                now = datetime.now().timestamp()\n                timeout = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n                dead_connections = [\n                    cid for cid, conn in self.connections.items()\n                    if (now - conn.last_heartbeat) > timeout\n                ]\n\n                for cid in dead_connections:\n                    logger.info(f\"Cleaning up dead connection {cid}\")\n                    # Send disconnect event before removing\n                    try:\n                        conn = self.connections[cid]\n                        conn.queue.put_nowait(SSEEvent(\n                            event_type=\"disconnect\",\n                            data={\"reason\": \"timeout\"},\n                        ))\n                    except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                        pass\n                    self.unregister(cid)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in cleanup loop: {e}\")\n\n\n# Global broadcaster instance\n_broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster | None = None\n\n\ndef get_broadcaster() -> SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n    global _broadcaster\n    if _broadcaster is None:\n        _broadcaster = SSEBroadcaster()\n    return _broadcaster\n\n\n# Event types\nclass EventType:\n    \"\"\"SSE event types.\"\"\"\n\n    # Connection lifecycle\n    CONNECTED = \"connected\"\n    DISCONNECT = \"disconnect\"\n    HEARTBEAT = \"heartbeat\"\n\n    # Result events\n    RESULT_CREATED = \"result_created\"\n    RESULT_UPDATED = \"result_updated\"\n\n    # Intent events\n    INTENT_PENDING = \"intent_pending\"\n    INTENT_DISPATCHED = \"intent_dispatched\"\n    INTENT_RESOLVED = \"intent_resolved\"\n\n    # Topic events\n    TOPIC_CREATED = \"topic_created\"\n    TOPIC_UPDATED = \"topic_updated\"\n    TOPIC_STALE = \"topic_stale\"\n\n    # Workload events\n    WORKLOAD_SUMMARY = \"workload_summary\"\n    EXCEPTION_RAISED = \"exception_raised\"\n\n    # Bead events\n    BEAD_CLOSED = \"bead_closed\"\n    BEAD_FAILED = \"bead_failed\"\n\n\nasync def broadcast_result(\n    result: dict,\n    session_id: str,\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"\n    Broadcast a result to relevant surfaces.\n\n    Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=EventType.RESULT_CREATED,\n        data=result,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n        target_surface_id=target_surface_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n\n\nasync def broadcast_intent_update(\n    intent: dict,\n    session_id: str,\n    event_type: str = EventType.INTENT_RESOLVED,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Broadcast an intent status update.\"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=event_type,\n        data=intent,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n    )\n    return At startup, `src/main.py` reads it via `tomllib` and passes it to FastAPI — the canvas header badge reads from `/openapi.json` which reflects this. **Do not hardcode the version anywhere else.**\n\n### Scheme: semantic versioning (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`)\n\n- `PATCH` — bug fixes, pipeline corrections, prompt tweaks\n- `MINOR` — new features, new intent types, new fetch sources, UI additions\n- `MAJOR` — architectural changes, breaking API changes\n\n### Release checklist\n\n1. Edit `pyproject.toml` — bump `version`\n2. Commit: `git commit -m \"chore: bump to vX.Y.Z\"`\n3. Tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z`\n4. Push: `git push origin main --tags`\n\nThere is no CI build for adc — it runs directly from source. The tag is the release.\n\n## Architecture\n\n```\nutterance (text or voice)\n  → POST /dispatch\n  → intent router (ZAI LLM classify)\n  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n  → broadcast SSEEvent(event_type=\"result_created\", target_surface_id=...)\n  → canvas SSE listener calls loadTopics()\n  → GET /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics → cards rendered\n```\n\nKey files:\n- `src/main.py` — FastAPI app, `/dispatch` endpoint, SSE broadcaster wiring\n- `src/intent/router.py` — LLM classification → fetch+synthesize → store persistence\n- `src/fetch/commands.py` — fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures\n- `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` — concurrent fetch execution with streaming and coverage tracking (FetchStrand implementation)\n- `src/synthesize/strand.py` — LLM synthesis into structured result\n- `src/session/store.py` — SQLite session store (aiosqlite); `data/session.db`\n- `src/sse/broadcaster.py` — SSE connection registry and event routing\n- `src/canvas/index.html` — single-page canvas UI\n\n## ZAI Proxy\n\nAll LLM calls go through the ZAI proxy at:\n\n```\nhttps://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages\n```\n\nSet via `ZAI_PROXY_URL` env var; default is the above. The vpn-wildcard-tls cert on the Traefik entrypoint is self-signed from Traefik's perspective — all httpx clients must use `verify=False`.\n\n**GLM-4.7 wraps all JSON responses in ` ```json ... ``` ` markdown fences.** Strip them before `json.loads()`:\n\n```python\nraw = response.strip()\nif raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n    raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n    raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\nresult = json.loads(raw)\n```\n\nThe proxy also wraps the Anthropic response envelope under a `\"result\"` key. Unwrap with `data.get(\"result\", data)` before accessing `content`/`usage`/`model`.\n\n## Session Store\n\n- `get_store()` is **synchronous** — do not `await` it\n- `find_or_create_topic()` returns `(topic_id: str, created: bool)` — always unpack\n- Topic `type` must be one of `('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')` — map intent types before passing\n- `create_utterance(session_id, raw_text, utterance_id=None)` — `utterance_id` is optional\n\n## SSE Broadcaster\n\nThere are **two SSEEvent classes** — do not mix them:\n\n- `src/sse/broadcaster.py` → `SSEEvent(event_type: str, data: dict, target_surface_id=...)` — **use this one** for broadcasting\n- `src/sse/events.py` → `SSEEvent(type: EventType, data: dict)` — legacy, used internally\n\nCanvas listens for `\"result_created\"` and `\"topic_updated\"` events. Runs locally on the Hetzner server at port 8000.\n\n## Running the Server\n\n```bash\n# From /home/coding/aide-de-camp/\nnohup python3 -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > /tmp/adc.log 2>&1 &\n\n# Restart (kill then start)\nkill -2 $(ps aux | grep \"uvicorn src.main\" | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}') 2>/dev/null; true\nnohup python3 -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > /tmp/adc.log 2>&1 &\n\n# Health check\ncurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nLogs go to `/tmp/adc.log`. Root logging is configured in `src/main.py` — all `src.*` module loggers are visible at INFO level.\n\n## Versioning\n\n**Every meaningful push must bump the version and cut a git tag.**\n\nVersion lives in `pyproject.toml` → `[project] version`. At startup, `src/main.py` reads it via `tomllib` and passes it to FastAPI — the canvas header badge reads from `/openapi.json` which reflects this. **Do not hardcode the version anywhere else.**\n\n### Scheme: semantic versioning (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`)\n\n- `PATCH` — bug fixes, pipeline corrections, prompt tweaks\n- `MINOR` — new features, new intent types, new fetch sources, UI additions\n- `MAJOR` — architectural changes, breaking API changes\n\n### Release checklist\n\n1. Edit `pyproject.toml` — bump `version`\n2. Commit: `git commit -m \"chore: bump to vX.Y.Z\"`\n3. Tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z`\n4. Push: `git push origin main --tags`\n\nThere is no CI build for adc — it runs directly from source. The tag is the release.\n\n## Architecture\n\n```\nutterance (text or voice)\n  → POST /dispatch\n  → intent router (ZAI LLM classify)\n  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n  → broadcast SSEEvent(event_type=\"result_created\", target_surface_id=...)\n  → canvas SSE listener calls loadTopics()\n  → GET /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics → cards rendered\n```\n\nKey files:\n- `src/main.py` — FastAPI app, `/dispatch` endpoint, SSE broadcaster wiring\n- `src/intent/router.py` — LLM classification → fetch+synthesize → store persistence\n- `src/fetch/commands.py` — fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures\n- `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` — concurrent fetch execution with streaming and coverage tracking (FetchStrand implementation)\n- `src/synthesize/strand.py` — LLM synthesis into structured result\n- `src/session/store.py` — SQLite session store (aiosqlite); `data/session.db`\n- `src/sse/broadcaster.py` — SSE connection registry and event routing\n- `src/canvas/index.html` — single-page canvas UI\n\n## ZAI Proxy\n\nAll LLM calls go through the ZAI proxy at:\n\n```\nhttps://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages\n```\n\nSet via `ZAI_PROXY_URL` env var; default is the above. The vpn-wildcard-tls cert on the Traefik entrypoint is self-signed from Traefik's perspective — all httpx clients must use `verify=False`.\n\n**GLM-4.7 wraps all JSON responses in ` ```json ... ``` ` markdown fences.** Strip them before `json.loads()`:\n\n```python\nraw = response.strip()\nif raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n    raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n    raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\nresult = json.loads(raw)\n```\n\nThe proxy also wraps the Anthropic response envelope under a `\"result\"` key. Unwrap with `data.get(\"result\", data)` before accessing `content`/`usage`/`model`.\n\n## Session Store\n\n- `get_store()` is **synchronous** — do not `await` it\n- `find_or_create_topic()` returns `(topic_id: str, created: bool)` — always unpack\n- Topic `type` must be one of `('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')` — map intent types before passing\n- `create_utterance(session_id, raw_text, utterance_id=None)` — `utterance_id` is optional\n\n## SSE Broadcaster\n\nThere are **two SSEEvent classes** — do not mix them:\n\n- `src/sse/broadcaster.py` → `SSEEvent(event_type: str, data: dict, target_surface_id=...)` — **use this one** for broadcasting\n- `src/sse/events.py` → `SSEEvent(type: EventType, data: dict)` — legacy, used internally\n\nCanvas listens for `\"result_created\"` and `\"topic_updated\"` events. Broadcast with:\n\n```python\nawait _broadcaster.broadcast(\n    SSEEvent(\n        event_type=\"result_created\",\n        target_surface_id=surface_id,\n        data={...}\n    )\n)\n```\n\n## Canvas Dispatch Contract\n\nThe canvas sends `surface_id` with every dispatch POST — required for SSE targeting:\n\n```javascript\nbody: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId })\n```\n\nThe canvas fetches topics from `/api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics` on SSE `result_created`.\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":95,"oldLines":15,"newStart":95,"newLines":13,"lines":[" "," ## SSE Broadcaster"," ","-There are **two SSEEvent classes** — do not mix them:","-","-- `src/sse/broadcaster.py` → `SSEEvent(event_type: str, data: dict, target_surface_id=...)` — **use this one** for broadcasting","-- `src/sse/events.py` → `SSEEvent(type: EventType, data: dict)` — legacy, used internally","-"," Canvas listens for `\"result_created\"` and `\"topic_updated\"` events. Plan context: docs/plan/plan.md hot path relies on SSE delivery ('Results streamed via SSE to active canvas'); a single event type keeps that path safe for autonomous modification.\n\nTasks:\n1.
